raw.sfdetails__c:
Support options

You can easily get support for NGINX software, either through the experts at NGINX Support Services or the broad community of NGINX users and contributors.

Community support

The following resources are operated and moderated by the NGINX community:

  • Community forum (community.nginx.org)
  • GitHub discussions (github.com/nginx/nginx/discussions)
  • NGINX on Kubernetes (kubernetes.nginx.org)

NGINX Support Services

NGINX Support Services remotely assists you with specific break-fix issues regarding ongoing maintenance of your active NGINX subscriptions.

Use of NGINX Open Source and NGINX solutions

NGINX Support Services is available for NGINX One Console, NGINX Plus, NGINX Ingress Controller, NGINX Instance Manager, F5 WAF for NGINX, NGINX Gateway Fabric, and NGINX as a Service for Azure. Each installation or usage of NGINX software must be covered by a corresponding support subscription.

NGINX Support Services is also available for NGINX Open Source, provided that an F5 Standard or F5 Premium support subscription is purchased for each NGINX Open Source instance requiring support. See Support policy for NGINX Open Source.

Software updates

Effective May 13, 2026, NGINX Plus and NGINX Ingress Controller adopted the following dual-release model, consisting of Long-Term Support (LTS) Releases and Continuous Releases (CR):

  • Long-Term Support (LTS) Releases: Annual releases for mission-critical production environments, focusing on stability and security. Each LTS release includes the latest security fixes and mitigation for CVEs, no additional features. Updates are provided for up to three years across up to three concurrent versions. An LTS release is identified by a 0 as the second digit in the version number, for example, PLS.37.0.0.1. An LTS release update increments the third digit, for example, PLS.37.0.1.1.
  • Continuous Releases (CR): Frequent releases within the annual LTS cycle that focus on latest features and performance improvements. Under this model, only the latest CR release is supported within your subscription and support terms. A CR release is identified by the second digit in the version number, for example, PLS.37.1.0.0, PLS.37.2.0.0, and so on. CR releases are never patched; instead, a new CR release is released. Both release types are immediately available through MyF5, with documentation available on NGINX Docs.

NGINX Open Source releases are immediately available on nginx.org, along with product documentation. You can obtain all other product releases through MyF5, with documentation available through NGINX Docs.

Debugging and error correction

If you experience an error, F5 works to fix it or provides a workaround. F5 responds to issues you raise and begins investigating critical issues within established Service Level Metrics (SLM).  F5 incorporates fixes into the next product update and adjusts the QA cycle for regression testing.

Security notifications

Security issues (such as Critical Errors and Vulnerabilities, or CVEs) are rare, but if they occur, F5 informs you about the implications and the solution directly, as quickly as possible.

Documentation discrepancies

If you are unsure about something in the documentation or something does not seem right, or if you have any questions, F5 strives to provide you with a precise response within the applicable SLM time frame.

Assistance with installation

NGINX technical support is not designed to walk you through the installation process. If you need comprehensive installation assistance, you may opt to work with the F5 Professional Services organization.

Support and services

F5 offers two levels of support for our customers across all products.

F5 support levels

Support policy for NGINX Open Source

F5 provides support services for NGINX Open Source software in accordance with the relevant portions of the F5 support policies and as described in this section. An F5 Standard or F5 Premium support subscription must be purchased for each NGINX Open Source instance requiring support.

Packages and modules covered by F5 Support

  • Linux packages of NGINX Open Source stable and mainline versions obtained directly from F5 NGINX.

    NGINX Open Source software support is provided when the following versions of the software are obtained directly:

    • Stable releases—The current and prior two main stable releases are supported, including minor releases for security issues.
    • Mainline releases—Releases published within the last two years are supported.
    • Modules distributed as part of NGINX Open Source are supported.

Security patches and bug fixes

The following are included with the subscription:

  • Security patches for the latest mainline and stable releases.
  • Critical bug fixes in the latest mainline release.
  • Non-critical bug fixes in the latest or a future mainline release.

Note: Patches and fixes are not backported.

Not covered by F5 Support

The following are not supported:

  • NGINX Open Source binaries compiled by anyone other than F5 NGINX.
  • Modules developed and/or compiled by anyone other than F5 NGINX.
  • NGINX Open Source prepackaged as part of an OS distribution.
  • NGINX products and third-party modules altered or modified by anyone other than F5 NGINX.
  • NGINX products and third-party modules used on or in conjunction with hardware or software other than as specified in official NGINX documentation.
  • Any custom scripts written on top of modules or to extend the functionality of these modules.
  • Defects in NGINX products and third-party modules due to accident, hardware malfunction, abuse, or improper use.
  • Versions of NGINX products and third-party modules for which support services have been discontinued by F5.
  • Errors caused by third-party modules not licensed through F5.
  • Evaluation software or other software provided by F5 at no charge, unless otherwise expressly provided in the associated license agreement.
  • Open source versions of other F5 products, unless otherwise expressly provided in the associated license agreement.
  • Products sold separately by F5.

SUPPORT FOR NGINX OPEN SOURCE SOFTWARE IS SUBJECT TO THE FURTHER CONDITIONS AND LIMITATIONS SET FORTH IN THE F5 MAINTENTANCE TERMS AND CONDITIONS, AS APPLICABLE, IT BEING UNDERSTOOD, FOR THE AVOIDANCE OF DOUBT, THAT F5 STRICTLY DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL WARRANTIES WITH RESPECT TO NGINX OPEN SOURCE SOFTWARE AND IS NOT UNDER ANY OBLIGATION TO REPAIR, REPLACE, UPGRADE, OR OTHERWISE MODIFY ANY SUCH SOFTWARE.

Frequently Asked Questions

What versions of NGINX Plus, NGINX Instance Manager, F5 WAF for NGINX, NGINX Gateway Fabric, and NGINX Ingress Controller are supported?

F5 advises users to run the most recent release of NGINX software, because we issue updates to only the most recent release. Security updates for NGINX Plus are issued as follows:

  • For the most recent and one prior release (for releases before NGINX Plus PLS.37.0.0.1 LTS)
  • For each LTS release within its lifecycle
  • As a new CR release (for releases after NGINX Plus PLS.37.0.0.1 LTS)

F5 proactively notifies all subscribers when updates are available. F5 provides technical support for the following:

  • Current release and releases that launched within two years of the launch date of the current release (for releases before NGINX Plus PLS.37.0.0.1 LTS).
  • Current LTS release
  • Latest CR release
  • LTS releases that launched within three years of the launch date of the current LTS Release (for releases after NGINX Plus PLS.37.0.0.1 LTS)

For release information, see the release notes for NGINX Plus, NGINX Plus Ingress Controller, NGINX Instance Manager, NGINX App Protect, and F5 WAF for NGINX, and NGINX Gateway Fabric.

How do I create a support ticket?

To create a support ticket, use the email addresses (for you and for NGINX engineering) in your support contract to send and receive support case information. With both F5 Standard and F5 Premium support subscriptions, you can get support by phone for critical issues.

How do I get support for NGINX software?

You can purchase a subscription to NGINX One Per Instance or to NGINX One for Kubernetes. For descriptions of each of these bundles, refer to F5 NGINX Products and Packaging.

What SLM can NGINX guarantee?

F5 Support adheres closely to our established SLM of 30 minutes for Severity 1 issues opened by phone. F5 will respond to Severity 1 response times to cases opened in MyF5 within one hour. Contact F5 Support to learn more. Agreements for guaranteed service levels are only available as part of specific individual support contracts between F5 and the customer and are not provided automatically as part of maintenance and support.

How is the Open Source product supported?

NGINX supports binary builds of NGINX Open Source that are obtained from the nginx.org repositories. Purchase an F5 Standard or F5 Premium support subscription for each NGINX Open Source instance that requires support, and use a prebuilt NGINX Open Source binary instead of an NGINX Plus binary. NGINX does not support binaries that you build from source yourself or binary distributions obtained from other sources.

How does NGINX support third-party modules?

NGINX provides commercially reasonable effort support for the optional third-party modules that we build and maintain. A list of these modules is available on our Technical Specifications page. Support coverage may be limited to one hour per query and referred to F5 Professional Services if necessary.

F5 does not support custom or third-party modules that are not listed on our Technical Specifications page. F5 may ask you to disable such modules before providing support.

What happens when my support expires?

After your support contract expires, you are no longer licensed to use NGINX Plus or obtain support from NGINX. Access to NGINX Plus updates will be prohibited, and you must stop and delete your NGINX Plus instances. Contact F5 Support to renew your NGINX Plus subscription.

How can I get design or consultancy help?

If you need help with architecture, design, or configuration, our Professional Services team can help you translate your requirements into an optimized NGINX configuration. Contact F5 Support to learn more.

How can I get proactive notifications of new releases and security updates?

Register users at the MyF5 homepage. F5 emails all details of new NGINX releases and any critical security updates to these users. For more information about subscribing to notifications, refer to K9970: Subscribe to customer security announcement emails regarding F5 products. Please also refer to the NGINX product documentation, which is updated regularly. Users of NGINX Amplify can also find version and security advisory information in their system reports.

raw.sfdetails__c:
Policy Information

F5 adheres to industry standard software practices and conducts third-party and internal code reviews as part of the F5 secure software development lifecycle. F5 is committed to responding quickly to potential vulnerabilities in F5 products and services and appreciates the opportunity to work with F5 customers, independent and corporate researchers, industry representatives, and network/application security professionals, to identify and resolve potential vulnerabilities.

This article defines the F5 security vulnerability response policy.

  • Reporting suspected vulnerabilities
  • Vulnerability categories
  • Third-party software components
  • Security advisories
  • Vulnerability disclosure policy
  • Security hotfixes
  • Receiving security vulnerability information
  • Important links
  • F5 SIRT PGP key

Reporting suspected vulnerabilities

F5 welcomes any reports of suspected vulnerabilities or other security concerns with F5 products, services, or web properties.

If you are an F5 customer with an active support contract, contact F5 Support.

If you are not an F5 customer, please use the form at Report a Vulnerability.

In cases where responsible disclosure is followed, and at the reporter's request, F5 provides attribution to reporters within a public MyF5 article.

Vulnerability categories

F5 investigates and prioritizes vulnerability reports based on the potential exploitability of the system and communicates the vulnerability impact using Common Vulnerability Scoring System version 3.1 (CVSS v3.1). When F5 determines the Severity (Low, Medium, High, or Critical), the CVSS v3.1 score is used.

F5 uses the following guidelines to categorize and document vulnerabilities.

CVSS scoreSeverity of vulnerability
9.0 - 10.0Critical
7.0 - 8.9High
4.0 - 6.9Medium
0.1 - 3.9Low

F5 assigns Common Vulnerability and Exposures (CVE) identifiers and publishes security advisories for all vulnerability categories, from Low to Critical.

Starting with the August 2024 Quarterly Security Notification, F5 will also provide a CVSS v4.0 base score for first-party security issues, displayed below the CVSS v3.1 score. For example:

ProductBranchVersions known to be vulnerable1Fixes introduced inSeverity/CVSS score2Vulnerable component or feature
BIG-IP (all modules)21.x21.0.021.0.1Low/2.0 (CVSS v3.1)
Low/1.8 (CVSS v4.0)		Component

For third-party issues, F5 will continue to provide only CVSS v3.1 scores, while we build experience with CVSS v4.0. For more information about how F5 uses CVSS v4.0, refer to K000140363: Overview of CVSS v4.0 in F5 security advisories.

Additionally, F5 may publish security advisories for security exposures, which do not affect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA) of the F5 product, but may impact the CIA of one of the following:

  • Services or network traffic processed by the F5 product
  • Confidentiality of data cached on the F5 product

Security exposures are generally dependent on the configuration and use of the F5 device. Therefore, F5 cannot produce an accurate CVSS score or severity for security exposures. F5 recommends that you treat security exposures as a high priority and evaluate the specific severity of these issues in your own environment.

F5 reserves the right to deviate from this policy as deemed appropriate, for example, to ensure that F5 considers risks that are not captured in the CVSS v3.1 score.

For more information about CVSS v3.1, visit www.first.org/cvss.

Note: The previous link takes you to a resource outside of MyF5. The third party could remove the document without our knowledge.

Third-party software components

For third-party software components that F5 products and services use, F5 evaluates the vulnerability risk based on how F5 uses the third-party component and calculates a CVSS v3.1 score that reflects the impact to F5 products or services. The BIG-IP system is not a general-purpose computing platform, and the system implements strict access control as well as strict control when it comes to using third-party components. Even in cases where potentially vulnerable code is present, the F5 product might never use the vulnerable function, or root/admin access might be required for anybody to exploit the system.

F5 proactively investigates vulnerabilities in specific third-party packages (OpenSSL, Apache, Apache Tomcat, and bind). For these packages, we will investigate the impact of any disclosed CVE regardless of whether it was disclosed in the version running on the F5 product or a later version and regardless of the support status of the third-party package with the vendor (for example, Apache Foundation, ISC).

For other third-party packages (for example, log4j, node.js, and MySQL), F5 evaluates vulnerabilities when requested to do so through a customer case but does not proactively evaluate vulnerabilities. When requested, however, F5 evaluates them in the same way as we do for proactively investigated vulnerabilities.

For more information and examples, refer to K000133035: How F5 evaluates vulnerabilities in third-party packages.

Security advisories

F5 uses security advisories to communicate security information to F5 customers regarding vulnerabilities and security exposures. Security advisories provide detailed information such as applicability, impact, and mitigation options regarding security issues that involve F5 both directly and indirectly.

For information on the type of issue and content found in security advisories, refer to K41942608: Overview of MyF5 security advisory articles.

Vulnerability disclosure policy

F5 is committed to responsibly disclosing information that contains sufficient detail about the vulnerability in question, such as the CVSS score and vector. This information is intended to help customers understand the impact, severity, potential mitigations, and software fixes related to the vulnerability when such information is available.

F5 does not provide the following information:

  • Example exploit code or reproduction information
  • The number of customers or sites affected
  • Any information F5 regards as confidential

We encourage customers to keep their software as current as is feasible to benefit from the latest fixes.

F5 discloses vulnerabilities and security exposures across products and services in a Quarterly Security Notification. Dates for these notifications are scheduled in advance; refer to K12201527: Overview of Quarterly Security Notifications to view the schedule for the Quarterly Security Notifications. When needed, and to ensure the protection of our customers’ systems, F5 may issue Security Alerts to communicate vulnerability information outside of the Quarterly Security Notifications.

Security hotfixes

When a vulnerability is publicly disclosed, F5 is committed to evaluating the software versions that have not yet reached End of Technical Support (EoTS), as indicated in the software support policy articles for each product. For more information, refer to K8986: F5 product support policies.

When critical vulnerabilities are discovered, F5 implements, tests, and releases security hotfixes for the supported software versions that have not yet reached End of Software Development (EoSD), where technically feasible. When critical vulnerabilities are discovered for software versions that have reached EoSD, but have not yet reached EoTS, F5 recommends upgrading to a version that has not yet reached EoSD to receive the hotfix.

Note: F5 does not evaluate software versions that have reached End of Technical Support (EoTS).

Receiving security vulnerability information

Customers can stay connected and receive the latest security information from F5 by referring to the following links:

  • K9957: Creating a custom RSS feed to view new and updated documents
  • K9970: Subscribe to email notifications regarding F5 products and security announcements
  • F5 Labs

Important links

  • About the F5 SIRT
  • About Platform Security

raw.sfdetails__c:
Policy Information

MyF5 security advisory articles may document the following:

  • A vulnerability or multiple vulnerabilities
  • A security exposure issue
  • Security related topics

Security vulnerability

Security advisory articles describing vulnerabilities refer to the issue as a vulnerability and include the CVE or VU number when appropriate.

For more information about vulnerability definitions and the response policy, refer to K4602: Overview of the F5 security vulnerability response policy.

Security exposure

Security advisory articles describing a security exposure refer to the ID number tracked by F5 Product Development.

F5 publishes security advisories for security exposures, which do not affect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA) of the F5 product, but may impact the CIA of one of the following:

  • Services or network traffic processed by the F5 product
  • Confidentiality of data cached on the F5 product

Security exposures are generally dependent on configuration and use of the F5 device. Therefore, F5 cannot produce an accurate CVSS score or severity for security exposures. F5 recommends customers treat security exposures as a high priority and evaluate the specific severity of these issues in their own environment.

For more information about F5's response to security exposures, refer to K4918: Overview of the F5 critical issue hotfix policy.

Security related topics

F5 categorizes articles related to security as security advisories. This categorization enables customers to easily locate the information in search results and RSS options. For example, if a customer selects Security Advisory under Content Type to filter MyF5 search results, the refined search results will include security related topics as well as security vulnerabilities and exposures.

For example, F5 categorized the following article as a security advisory: K000160327: Protect your network from geopolitical uncertainty with F5.
